page: Microsoft Azure F48als_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Falsv6 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 server features 48 physical cores and 48 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. It is configured with 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The processor includes a cache hierarchy of 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache, with hardware support for advanced vector instructions such as AVX-512 and AVX512_BF16.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier performance in compute-heavy workloads. The server achieves top 10% placement in stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, as well as multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench multi-core evaluations. Software compilation with Clang and image processing also rank in the top 10%. Memory bandwidth is strong, placing in the top 25% with read-only cached speeds of 7124.96 GB/sec and uncached read speeds of 226.93 GB/sec. Large-model LLM inference drops to average performance, while smaller model text generation remains in the top tier.

The F48als_v6 does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, though it is premium storage capable. This configuration represents a highly cost-efficient profile for pure compute tasks where massive memory allocations are unnecessary. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it less suitable for memory-intensive databases but highly effective for compute-bound workloads. Appropriate applications include software compilation, image processing, high-throughput web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure F48als_v6 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server from the Falsv6 family, featuring 48 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 physical cores and vCPUs running at 3.7 GHz. It is equipped with 96.0 GB of memory, establishing a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports premium storage. Benchmarks show top-tier performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng CPU tests, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, software compilation, and image processing. Memory bandwidth is strong, reaching 7124.96 GB/sec for cached reads. This resource profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy tasks that do not require large memory footprints. It is highly suited for workloads such as software compilation, web serving, and lightweight LLM inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F48als_v6 is a compute-optimized server featuring 48 AMD EPYC cores, 96 GB of RAM, and top-tier CPU performance. It is designed for software compilation, image processing, and web serving.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F48als_v6 is a compute-optimized server with 48 AMD EPYC cores and 96 GB RAM, delivering top-tier performance for compute-heavy tasks.
tagline: A high-frequency, 48-core AMD EPYC compute-optimized instance designed for intensive processing workloads with a lean memory footprint.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 cores running at 3.7 GHz
96.0 GB system memory with a 2.0 GB per core ratio
Top-tier performance in CPU benchmarks and software compilation
No local storage or GPU, but premium storage capable
Suited for compute-bound tasks, web serving, and light inference
